channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Fletcher's remarkable Scotland World Cup call after 17 minutes for Man Utd
item.description: Tyler Fletcher was expecting to go to the World Cup as a fan this summer - now he could be playing in it for Scotland.

item.title: 'He wanted to take it' - Gabriel's first Arsenal penalty ends with heartbreak
item.description: Gabriel's first penalty for Arsenal ends in heartache as his shootout miss hands Paris St-Germain a second successive Champions League title.

item.title: Uruguay pick Man Utd's Ugarte but Suarez left out
item.description: Uruguay include Manchester United midfielder Manuel Ugarte in their squad for the World Cup - but there is no place for veteran striker Luis Suarez.

item.title: PSG retain Champions League with penalty shootout win over Arsenal
item.description: Arsenal lose a penalty shootout as Paris St-Germain win the Champions League for a second successive year.

item.title: PSG go back-to-back and join 'greatest of all time'
item.description: Paris St-Germain underline their status as one of European football's greatest ever teams by retaining their Champions League crown with victory against Arsenal.

item.title: Arsenal 'pain' will fuel fire after Champions League heartbreak
item.description: On Sunday, Arsenal will get on an open bus parade to celebrate their Premier League title less than 24 hours after their Champions League final heartbreak.

item.title: Slot sacking completes a remarkable fall from grace
item.description: 'Slot's is one of the most remarkable falls from grace in Premier League history' says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

item.title: Gabriel blazes penalty over as PSG win Champions League
item.description: Paris St-Germain retain their Champions League crown as Arsenal defender Gabriel misses the decisive spot-kick in a penalty shootout.

item.title: Scotland set off for World Cup - in pictures
item.description: Photographs from Scotland's World Cup departure, with Steve Clarke's squad on their way to the USA.

item.title: England players advised to holiday in US pre-World Cup
item.description: England head coach Thomas Tuchel wants his players to get used to the weather and US time zones well before they kick-off their World Cup campaign on 17 June.

item.title: Hundreds arrested and police injured after Champions League riots in France
item.description: Nearly 800 people were arrested after clashes with police which saw 219 people injured, including 57 police officers.

item.title: Fletcher chosen to replace Gilmour in Scotland squad
item.description: Manchester United teenager Tyler Fletcher is a late replacement for the injured Billy Gilmour in Scotland's World Cup squad.

item.title: Glasner considered for AC Milan job - Sunday's gossip
item.description: Oliver Glasner considered for AC Milan job, while Liverpool negotiate with Inter Milan on a price for Curtis Jones, plus more.

item.title: Shankland and Curtis make World Cup move - but will Clarke respond?
item.description: Lawrence Shankland and Findlay Curtis were the big winners in Scotland's friendly win over Curacao, writes Tom English.

item.title: Decision not to award Arsenal penalty correct - analysis
item.description: Champions League analysts Nedum Onuoha and Pat Nevin both feel the decision to not award Arsenal a penalty in extra-time of the Champions League final against PSG was the correct one.

item.title: Dembele equalises from penalty spot for PSG
item.description: PSG's Ousmane Dembele scores from the penalty spot to level the Champions League final against Arsenal in Budapest.

item.title: How Kvaratskhelia shook off Arsenal shackles in second half
item.description: Champions League analysts Nedum Onuoha and Pat Nevin look at how Khvicha Kvaratskhelia was able to beat Arsenal's plan to stifle him to become influential in the second half of the Champions League final.

item.title: Havertz fires Arsenal into early lead against PSG
item.description: Kai Havertz fires Arsenal into an early lead in the Champions League final against Paris St-Germain in Budapest.

item.title: Watch Scotland put four past Curacao in World Cup send-off
item.description: Watch Scotland come from behind to beat 10-man Curacao in their World Cup send-off at Hampden.

item.title: Can you name every Champions League winner?
item.description: Only the most knowledgeable of football fans will ace this quiz.

item.title: Rabonas & fake births - Chelsea win World Sevens
item.description: The Women's World Sevens series in London had it all from backflips, rabonas and even a fake birth.

item.title: Sterling arrested on suspicion of drug-driving
item.description: Raheem Sterling has been arrested on suspicion of drug-driving after he allegedly crashed his car into motorway barriers.

item.title: A team in need of change - why Liverpool parted with Slot
item.description: Why did Liverpool call time on head coach Arne Slot after two seasons in charge in which the club won the Premier League title?

item.title: What now for Vinicius Jr after Mourinho signs Real Madrid deal?
item.description: How Jose Mourinho manages his relationship with Brazil forward Vinicius Junior will be one of the most closely watched aspects of his second spell in charge of Real Madrid.

item.title: South Africa made to look like fools over World Cup visa issues, says minister
item.description: The country's football association says it is working to resolve the problems that have delayed the team's departure to Mexico.

item.title: How Slot went from champion to sack in a year
item.description: Just one year after lifting Liverpool's 20th league title in his debut season, Arne Slot has been sacked. BBC Sport looks back on how a dream start unravelled on Merseyside.

item.title: The Sunderland footballer setting record straight after 46 years
item.description: When Roly Gregoire quit football in 1980 after suffering a knee injury, he had faced so much racism that he could not bear to watch football for many years.

item.title: Spurs secure Premier League survival with win over Everton
item.description: Joao Palhinha's goal just before half time helps Tottenham overcome Everton to secure the result they needed to ensure Premier League survival on a day of nerve-shredding tension.

item.title: West Ham relegated despite win over Leeds
item.description: West Ham's 14-year stay in the Premier League comes to an end after they were relegated to the Championship on the final day of the season despite beating Leeds at London Stadium.

item.title: Arsenal beat Palace before lifting Premier League trophy
item.description: Arsenal start their reign as Premier League champions with a routine win over Crystal Palace in a party atmosphere at Selhurst Park.

item.title: Sunderland beat Chelsea to qualify for Europa League
item.description: Sunderland beat Chelsea to qualify for European competition for the first time in 53 years, securing a place in the Europa League at the visitors' expense.

item.title: Fernandes breaks Premier League record as Man Utd beat Brighton
item.description: Manchester United beat Brighton on the final day of the season, with captain Bruno Fernandes breaking the Premier League record for most assists in a single campaign.

item.title: World Cup: Every squad as they are announced
item.description: Every squad for the 2026 World Cup in the US, Canada and Mexico as they are announced.

item.title: Liverpool sack head coach Slot and approach Iraola
item.description: Liverpool sack head coach Arne Slot after two seasons in charge, with Andoni Iraola the leading contender to replace him.

item.title: O'Dwyer, 16, added to Republic of Ireland squad as training player
item.description: Shamrock Rovers teenager Josh O'Dwyer is included in the Republic of Ireland squad for their friendly against World Cup co-hosts Canada in Montreal.

item.title: Davies in 'best ever' Canada squad for World Cup
item.description: Head coach Jesse Marsch includes Bayern Munich defender Alphonso Davies in what he describes as the best Canada squad "ever assembled" for this summer's World Cup.

item.title: What is Scotland's most iconic goal? The final - Gemmill v McTominay
item.description: Vote to find out what goal will be crowned Scotland's greatest.

item.title: Rice, Shaw and McInnes among BBC Football Awards winners
item.description: For the first time, BBC Sport is handing out end-of-season football awards to the cream of the crop in English and Scottish football - and here are the winners.

item.title: U-turns, tension and trophies - inside 12 glorious months of Glasner
item.description: Crystal Palace's Conference League victory comes at the end of a remarkable 12-month period of success under departing boss Oliver Glasner.

item.title: The most iconic Champions League final performances ranked
item.description: Steven Gerrard in Istanbul? Gareth Bale's double? Teddy Sheringham or Ole Gunnar Solskjaer? Ranking the best Champions League final performances.

item.title: Unsung hero? Biggest disappointment? Fans rate your club's season
item.description: BBC Sport's Premier League club fan writers assess their team's performance as the domestic season comes to an end.

item.title: Premier League predictions - how accurate were BBC Sport pundits?
item.description: Last summer, 33 BBC pundits - plus AI and Opta's 'supercomputer' were asked to pick which teams would finish in the Premier League's top four, and in which order. How did they get on?

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: How much prize money have Premier League teams earned in Europe?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how much Arsenal, Aston Villa and Crystal Palace have earned in Europe this season

item.title: Which club received most red cards in a Premier League season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the teams who have received the most red cards in a Premier League season.

item.title: Who won the Premier League golden boot?
item.description: BBC's Ask Me Anything team explains who has won the Premier League golden boot and other individual awards.

item.title: What would Crystal Palace winning Conference League mean for English clubs in Europe?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how Crystal Palace winning the Conference League would effect English teams entering Europe next season

item.title: Who are the oldest players to feature at a World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who the oldest players are in the history of the World Cup

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League assists in a season?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at who has provided the most assists in the history of the Premier League.

item.title: Shankland shines, familiar flaws & Haiti warning - what we learned from Scotland win
item.description: There was plenty to ponder from Scotland's 4-1 win over 10-man Curacao at Hampden on Saturday, in what was their send-off before heading to America.

item.title: Brighton's Vidosic hopes to honour late father in FA Cup final
item.description: Brighton manager Dario Vidosic is hoping to honour his late father in Sunday's Women's FA Cup final when they take on Manchester City.

item.title: Seagulls fans flock to Wembley for FA Cup final
item.description: Fans head to London before Brighton & Hove Albion take on Man City in the Women's FA Cup final.

item.title: Wales boss Wilkinson has unfinished business
item.description: Rhian Wilkinson says she extended her spell as Wales head coach because she has unfinished business in the role.

item.title: Unmasking the coach plotting Man City's quest for a domestic double
item.description: Who is the Swedish mastermind who has guided Manchester City to a first Women's Super League title for a decade?

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.
